rules.message: Do not use os.tmpdir() for temporary files that AI agents will read — MCP clients enforce workspace boundaries. Write temp files inside the project directory (e.g., .totem/temp/) instead.
Do not use fs.createWriteStream() as a stdio argument for spawn() — the fd is null until the async 'open' event fires. Use fs.openSync(path, 'a') instead to get a synchronous file descriptor.

Use nullish coalescing (??) instead of logical OR (||) when defaulting numeric metrics (latency, token counts, etc.). Logical OR incorrectly replaces valid 0 values (e.g., cached responses) with the fallback.
Regex for URL/input parsing should be anchored with ^ and include protocol (https?://). Unanchored regexes match substrings, which is rarely the intent for CLI input parsing.
LanceDB SQL uses backticks for column identifiers, not double quotes. Double-quoted camelCase column names silently return zero matches. Use backticks: `columnName` instead of "columnName".
Use @clack/prompts instead of inquirer for CLI interactions — it provides a modern, premium visual style that supports the Magic Onboarding goal.

Use dynamic import for 'ora' (e.g., const { default: ora } = await import('ora')) inside the function that needs it, rather than a top-level static import. This avoids adding startup cost to every CLI command.
Do not use $TOOL_INPUT directly in shell commands like echo — treat it as untrusted data to prevent command injection. Use printf '%s' or quote and validate the variable.
Do not use fire-and-forget (void) for side-effect operations during tool execution. Always await to confirm success or failure.
Escape closing XML tags in LLM prompts using backslash escaping (e.g., `<\/tag>`) instead of unescaped `</tag>` to prevent prompt injection
Prefer dynamic imports inside command function bodies rather than at module scope to preserve lazy loading and fast CLI startup

Combine `typeof val === 'object'` with a truthiness check (`val && typeof val === 'object'`) to guard against null — `typeof null` returns 'object' and accessing properties on null causes runtime crashes.
Do not expand GitHub Actions inputs directly in run steps — map inputs to intermediate environment variables first to prevent command injection vulnerabilities
Environment variable 'configured' checks must validate non-whitespace content using /\S/.test(process.env.VAR) to avoid false positives from empty or whitespace-only values
Use exact count assertions (toBe(N)) instead of weak bounds (toBeGreaterThanOrEqual) for fixed collections — prevents silent regression when items are accidentally deleted.

For background maintenance tasks with small expected workloads, prefer sequential for...of loops over Promise.all. Sequential processing simplifies per-item error isolation without needing Promise.allSettled.

rules.lessonHeading: Sanitize user-provided text before persisting to files
rules.message: Sanitize ANSI escape sequences from user-provided content before writing to files. Use a helper that strips ESC sequences (e.g., /\x1b\[[0-9;]*m/g) before persisting to Markdown files or logs.

rules.lessonHeading: When re-throwing errors in a CLI orchestrator, always
rules.message: Re-thrown errors in the CLI orchestrator must include the standard '[Totem Error]' prefix for consistent user experience

rules.lessonHeading: When timing out a child process, do not reject the promise
rules.message: Do not reject the promise directly inside setTimeout when killing a child process. Call child.kill() in setTimeout and reject inside the 'close' event handler to ensure stdio streams are fully flushed.

rules.lessonHeading: Synchronous execSync with piped stdio can cause the parent
rules.message: Avoid execSync with piped stdio — use async spawn with manual stream collection to prevent parent process hangs or silent aborts due to pipe buffer limits.

rules.lessonHeading: The Git -- separator treats all subsequent arguments
rules.message: Revision specifiers (like branch...HEAD or branch..HEAD) must appear before the '--' separator in git commands, not after it. Arguments after '--' are treated as file paths.

rules.lessonHeading: When using the @google/genai SDK (v1+), the constructor
rules.message: The @google/genai SDK (v1+) requires an options object: new GoogleGenAI({ apiKey }) — not a raw string. The string-only constructor is from the older @google/generative-ai package.

rules.lessonHeading: When normalizing diverse SDK errors for internal retry
rules.message: Do not create a new Error instance when normalizing SDK errors for retry logic. Instead, mutate the original error's .name property and re-throw it to preserve the stack trace and provider-specific metadata.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid refactoring synchronous factory functions to async
rules.message: Avoid making factory functions async just to hoist dynamic import() calls. Node.js caches dynamic imports after first invocation — keep the factory synchronous to avoid propagating await through the call chain.

rules.lessonHeading: Use path.relative(process.cwd(),
rules.message: Use path.relative(process.cwd(), path.resolve(process.cwd(), input)) instead of string replacement with process.cwd() — handles shell-expanded absolute paths correctly

rules.lessonHeading: Use vi.importActual in mocks to preserve utility functions
rules.message: When mocking modules, use vi.importActual to preserve real utility function implementations. Re-implementing utility logic inside vi.mock makes tests brittle.

rules.lessonHeading: Prefer Vitest's expect().rejects.toHaveProperty()
rules.message: Prefer expect().rejects.toHaveProperty() over try/catch with expect.fail() — the latter can cause tests to accidentally pass if no error is thrown

rules.lessonHeading: Ensure all thrown errors, including those for missing
rules.message: All thrown errors must include the '[Totem Error]' prefix in the message for consistent error reporting.

rules.lessonHeading: Setting open-pull-requests-limit to 0 in dependabot.yml
rules.message: Setting open-pull-requests-limit to 0 suppresses all Dependabot PRs including routine bumps. Ensure this is intentional and that GitHub repo-level security settings are configured to still allow security patches.

rules.lessonHeading: Performing content.split('\n') inside a loop over line
rules.message: Avoid calling content.split('\n') inside a loop — hoist the split outside the loop to prevent O(N×M) quadratic complexity and potential DoS on large files.

rules.lessonHeading: Provide a dummy fallback API key (e.g., 'local-only')
rules.message: Provide a dummy fallback API key (e.g., 'local-only') instead of an empty string when targeting OpenAI-compatible local endpoints, to bypass client-side SDK validation.

rules.lessonHeading: Sanitize git-sourced metadata like branch names, status,
rules.message: Sanitize git command output to remove ANSI escape sequences and control characters before passing to downstream tools or LLM contexts (e.g., strip with /[\x00-\x1F\x7F]|\x1B\[[0-9;]*[mGKHF]/g)

rules.lessonHeading: Ollama num_ctx and VRAM: The OpenAI-compatible API adapter
rules.message: num_ctx in an object literal may be intended for Ollama, but the OpenAI-compatible API adapter silently ignores it. Use Ollama's native /api/chat endpoint with options: { num_ctx } instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Casting an object literal to an Error type does not satisfy
rules.message: Casting an object literal to Error does not satisfy instanceof Error checks at runtime. Use Object.assign(new Error(message), properties) instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using Zod's .url() validator for configurations where
rules.message: Avoid using Zod's .url() validator for host/endpoint configs — users often provide bare hostnames or host:port without a protocol, which fails strict URL validation. Consider using z.string() with a custom refinement instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using exit 0 inside git hooks intended for chaining
rules.message: Avoid 'exit 0' in git hooks — it terminates the shell process and prevents chained hooks from executing. Use if/fi blocks instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Detect existing hook managers and provide manual guidance
rules.message: Do not write directly to .git/hooks — detect existing hook managers (Husky, Lefthook) and provide manual integration guidance instead

rules.lessonHeading: When detecting Bun environments, check for both bun.lockb
rules.message: When detecting Bun environments, check for both 'bun.lockb' (legacy) and 'bun.lock' (Bun >= 1.2). Add a check for both lockfile names to ensure compatibility.

rules.lessonHeading: Ensure model name validation regexes explicitly allow
rules.message: Model name validation regex may not allow dot (.) delimiters — ensure patterns like /^[a-zA-Z0-9_.:-]+$/ accommodate newer naming schemes such as 'gpt-5.4'

rules.lessonHeading: Use pnpm exec for workspace binaries in monorepos
rules.message: Use 'pnpm exec' instead of 'pnpm bin' for workspace binary resolution in Turborepo monorepos — 'pnpm bin' often fails to locate binaries from internal workspace packages.

rules.lessonHeading: Resolve git root via rev-parse for monorepo compatibility
rules.message: Do not check for .git directory directly. Use 'git rev-parse --show-toplevel' to resolve the git root for monorepo/submodule/worktree compatibility.

rules.lessonHeading: Always iterate through all regex matches (e.g.,
rules.message: Use matchAll() instead of match() for security validations — match() only finds the first result, allowing attackers to shadow malicious payloads with a safe prefix.

rules.lessonHeading: Check for the presence of the 'g' flag before appending it
rules.message: Do not blindly append 'g' flag when constructing a RegExp — check if the flag is already present to avoid a runtime SyntaxError.

rules.lessonHeading: Use granular assertions rather than snapshots when testing
rules.message: Use granular assertions instead of snapshots when testing system prompts — snapshots break on minor prose changes and don't verify functional constraints

rules.lessonHeading: Always iterate through all regex matches (e.g.,
rules.message: Use matchAll() instead of match() for security validations — match() only finds the first result, allowing attackers to shadow malicious payloads with a safe prefix.

rules.lessonHeading: Check for the presence of the global ('g') flag
rules.message: Check for the presence of the 'g' flag before appending it. Re-adding 'g' to a pattern that already includes it causes a runtime SyntaxError.

rules.lessonHeading: Use the --recurse-submodules flag with git ls-files
rules.message: Use 'git ls-files --recurse-submodules' to ensure files in submodules are included in project scans

rules.lessonHeading: Changesets interactive CLI (pnpm changeset) crashes when
rules.message: Do not call 'pnpm changeset' interactively in automation — write changeset files manually to .changeset/ instead

rules.lessonHeading: Use --body-file for LLM text in CLI
rules.message: When passing LLM-generated text to CLI commands, use --body-file with a temp file instead of inline string arguments to prevent shell injection.

rules.lessonHeading: GitHub Actions shell injection via template expressions
rules.message: GitHub Actions shell injection risk: never use ${{ inputs.* }} or ${{ github.event.* }} directly in run: blocks. Map untrusted inputs to env: variables first, then reference them as "$VAR" in the shell script.

rules.lessonHeading: Provide a dummy fallback API key (e.g., 'local-only')
rules.message: Provide a dummy fallback API key (e.g., 'local-only')

rules.lessonHeading: Hooks designed to block agent actions, such as shield gates
rules.message: Shield gate hooks must use synchronous execution (execSync) to prevent race conditions. Using async exec() allows the agent to proceed before validation completes.

rules.lessonHeading: File deletions and mutations in cleanup or "eject" commands
rules.message: File deletions in cleanup/eject commands must be wrapped in try/catch so a single permission error doesn't abort the entire routine. Record failures as skipped items instead of throwing.

rules.lessonHeading: The Gemini CLI and Gemini Code Assist (GCA) do not
rules.message: Gemini CLI and GCA do not recognize .gemini/gemini.md. Use GEMINI.md (uppercase) at the project root instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Synchronous execSync with piped stdio can cause the parent
rules.message: Synchronous execSync with piped stdio can cause the parent

rules.lessonHeading: Swallowing errors in core modules hides silent failures,
rules.message: Swallowing errors in core modules hides silent failures,

rules.lessonHeading: Exclude 'pwd' from credential regexes
rules.message: Avoid 'pwd' in credential-scanning regexes — in Node.js it may refer to 'present working directory', causing false positives

rules.lessonHeading: Setting fail-fast: false in matrix workflows ensures
rules.message: Set fail-fast: false in matrix workflows to ensure all OS/environment runs complete even if one fails, providing full platform compatibility visibility.

rules.lessonHeading: Anchor command validation patterns with ^ to prevent hooks
rules.message: Command validation regex patterns should be anchored with ^ to prevent false positives when the command name appears as a substring in arguments

rules.lessonHeading: Replacing inline fs.rmSync calls with a shared helper
rules.message: Use the shared helper instead of inline fs.rmSync calls to ensure consistent retry logic and error-handling across the test suite.

rules.lessonHeading: Guard against self-suppressing patterns
rules.message: Patterns containing suppression directives (e.g., 'totem-ignore') are self-suppressing and logically unreachable — the engine strips these lines before evaluation.

rules.lessonHeading: When implementing custom glob matching for dir/\*/.ext,
rules.message: When implementing custom glob matching for dir/\*/.ext,

rules.lessonHeading: Use onWarn callbacks in core library modules
rules.message: Core library modules should not call console.warn() directly. Accept an optional onWarn callback parameter and invoke it instead, so callers control diagnostic output.

rules.lessonHeading: When detecting Bun environments, check for both bun.lockb
rules.message: When detecting Bun environments, check for both bun.lockb

rules.lessonHeading: Library code should use an onWarn callback instead
rules.message: Library code should not hardcode console.warn — accept an onWarn callback instead so consumers can route or suppress warnings

rules.lessonHeading: Hardcoded default values for properties like rule
rules.message: Hardcoded default values for properties like rule

rules.lessonHeading: Centralize error signature detection (such as 429 status
rules.message: Centralize error signature detection (such as 429 status

rules.lessonHeading: Including empty strings in branch whitelists can cause
rules.message: Do not include empty strings in branch whitelists — if branch resolution fails, an empty string will silently bypass security gates. Use explicit branch names only.

rules.lessonHeading: Pin Bun versions in CI
rules.message: Pin Bun to a specific version instead of 'latest' to prevent CI build drift from upstream releases.

rules.lessonHeading: SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers that call process.removeListener
rules.message: process.removeListener must reference the exact function object that was registered. Do not pass a function call expression — pass the variable holding the registered handler.

rules.lessonHeading: CLI command files should use dynamic await import()
rules.message: CLI command files should use dynamic `await import()` for heavy internal packages inside the command function, not top-level static imports. This keeps --help and version checks fast.

rules.lessonHeading: For local LLM providers, 500 Internal Server Errors often
rules.message: For local LLM providers, 500 Internal Server Errors often

rules.lessonHeading: Use a centralized cleanup helper with retry semantics
rules.message: Use a centralized cleanup helper with retry semantics (maxRetries: 3) instead of bare fs.rmSync for temporary directories, to prevent intermittent failures from Windows file locks.

rules.lessonHeading: When using optional diagnostic callbacks like 'onWarn?.()',
rules.message: Optional diagnostic callbacks like 'onWarn?.()' must have a fallback to 'console.warn' to prevent silent degradation when the callback is omitted.

rules.lessonHeading: Using child.kill() on Windows when shell: true is enabled
rules.message: child.kill() on Windows with shell:true leaves orphaned processes. Use taskkill /pid [pid] /T /F to terminate the entire process tree.

rules.lessonHeading: Use .min(1) on string schemas in config arrays
rules.message: Use z.array(z.string().min(1)) to reject empty strings at parse time instead of filtering them at runtime

rules.lessonHeading: Static top-level imports in CLI command files increase
rules.message: Avoid static top-level imports in CLI command files — use dynamic import() inside the command handler to reduce startup latency for commands like --help

rules.lessonHeading: Refrain from using console.warn within core library
rules.message: Do not use console.warn in core library packages — use the project-specific logger instead

rules.lessonHeading: Use xargs instead of 'tr' for joining multi-line outputs
rules.message: Use xargs instead of 'tr' for joining multi-line outputs into space-separated strings (POSIX compliance, consistent newline handling)

rules.lessonHeading: Standard console.log or console.error calls in MCP tools
rules.message: Standard console.log or console.error calls in MCP tools

rules.lessonHeading: Ollama num_ctx and VRAM: The OpenAI-compatible API adapter
rules.message: Ollama num_ctx and VRAM: The OpenAI-compatible API adapter

rules.lessonHeading: CWD-relative writes cause monorepo chaos
rules.message: Do not use process.cwd() to resolve file paths — resolve from the project root (config file location) instead to avoid orphaned artifacts in monorepo builds.

rules.lessonHeading: Use granular assertions rather than snapshots when testing
rules.message: Use granular assertions rather than snapshots when testing

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid blind catches in engine fallbacks
rules.message: Avoid blind catches in engine fallbacks — verify the error is specifically a LinkError or WASM initialization error before suppressing it, to avoid hiding unrelated architectural defects.

rules.lessonHeading: ESM intra-module mock requires re-binding
rules.message: ESM intra-module mock requires re-binding

rules.lessonHeading: When reading cache or config files, specifically check
rules.message: When reading cache or config files, specifically check

rules.lessonHeading: Lesson: Lesson headings are strictly limited to 60
rules.message: Lesson: Lesson headings are strictly limited to 60

rules.lessonHeading: Prefer [[ "$VAR" == "pattern"* ]] over piping to grep
rules.message: Prefer [[ "$VAR" == "pattern"* ]] over piping to grep — avoids spawning a subshell and is faster in git hooks

rules.lessonHeading: Standard console.log or console.error calls in MCP tools
rules.message: Do not use console.log in MCP tools — it corrupts the stdio transport protocol. Use a file-based logger or return a formatted system warning instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Use semantic constraints like "one to two short sentences"
rules.message: Use semantic constraints like "one to two short sentences"

rules.lessonHeading: Drift detector flags lesson file path mentions
rules.message: Drift detector flags lesson file path mentions

rules.lessonHeading: When determining if a session is interactive, check both
rules.message: When checking if a session is interactive, check both process.stdin.isTTY and process.stdout.isTTY to prevent ANSI codes/prompts when output is piped or redirected.

rules.lessonHeading: Using a unified "Totem Error" tag in error logs instead
rules.message: Using a unified "Totem Error" tag in error logs instead

rules.lessonHeading: Warning messages triggered by security violations
rules.message: Warning messages triggered by security violations

rules.lessonHeading: Repository conventions require the 'Totem Error' tag
rules.message: All log.error calls must include the 'Totem Error' tag for consistent diagnostic parsing and filtering

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid refactoring synchronous factory functions to async
rules.message: Avoid refactoring synchronous factory functions to async

rules.lessonHeading: Unconditionally deleting environment variables in test
rules.message: Do not unconditionally delete process.env variables in tests. Capture the original value before the test and restore it in cleanup (e.g., afterEach) to ensure proper test isolation.

rules.lessonHeading: Use pnpm exec for workspace binaries in monorepos
rules.message: Use pnpm exec for workspace binaries in monorepos

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using exit 0 inside git hooks intended for chaining
rules.message: Avoid using exit 0 inside git hooks intended for chaining

rules.lessonHeading: Dynamic imports intended for performance optimization
rules.message: Dynamic imports should only be used at CLI command entry points, not in utility or adapter layers. Move lazy-loading to the command entry point to maintain a clean dependency graph and avoid security scanner flags.

rules.lessonHeading: BSD sed on macOS does not support the \x1b hex escape; use
rules.message: BSD sed on macOS does not support \x1b hex escapes. Use perl or 'col -b' to strip ANSI color codes for cross-platform compatibility.

rules.lessonHeading: Using optional chaining for diagnostic callbacks
rules.message: Avoid optional chaining on diagnostic callbacks (e.g. onWarn?.()). Provide a fallback like `(onWarn ?? console.warn)(...)` to ensure critical failures are always visible.

rules.lessonHeading: Naive substring matching on paths, such as using
rules.message: Avoid naive substring matching on paths with .includes('adapters/'). Use regex boundaries or path.split() to avoid false matches like 'src/notadapters/'.

rules.lessonHeading: Hardcoding the CLI name or description in custom help
rules.message: Use cmd.name() and cmd.description() instead of hardcoding CLI name/description in help formatters

rules.lessonHeading: Using [val].flat().filter(Boolean) to normalize inputs
rules.message: Avoid [val].flat().filter(Boolean) — use explicit ternary checks (e.g., Array.isArray(val) ? val.filter(Boolean) : val ? [val] : []) to preserve TypeScript type narrowing and avoid generic array types.

rules.lessonHeading: Manually joining strings for YAML frontmatter can break
rules.message: Avoid manually joining strings to build YAML frontmatter — use a proper YAML serializer to handle special characters safely.

rules.lessonHeading: Prefer Vitest's expect().rejects.toHaveProperty()
rules.message: Prefer Vitest's expect().rejects.toHaveProperty()

rules.lessonHeading: When using the @google/genai SDK (v1+), the constructor
rules.message: When using the @google/genai SDK (v1+), the constructor

rules.lessonHeading: Using String() casting on input patterns can lead to silent
rules.message: Avoid String() casting on input patterns — it silently converts unexpected objects to '[object Object]'. Explicitly validate the type and throw a descriptive error instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Naive substring matching like includes('dir/') can trigger
rules.message: Naive substring matching with includes() on path strings can cause false positives on similarly named path segments. Use regex boundaries or path normalization (e.g., path.normalize, startsWith with separator) instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Always use fully qualified identifiers for caching
rules.message: Always use fully qualified identifiers for caching

rules.lessonHeading: In core packages, a small, tested custom implementation
rules.message: In core packages, a small, tested custom implementation

rules.lessonHeading: Validate JSON at system boundaries
rules.message: Avoid type assertions on JSON.parse results — perform element-level validation instead to prevent crashes from corrupted or manually edited files.

rules.lessonHeading: Functions designed to always throw an exception
rules.message: Functions designed to always throw should return 'never' instead of 'void' to inform TypeScript's control flow analysis.

rules.lessonHeading: Using domain-specific error subclasses instead of raw Error
rules.message: Throw a domain-specific error subclass instead of raw Error so CLI handlers can provide actionable recovery hints and suppress noisy stack traces for known failure modes.

rules.lessonHeading: Hooks designed to block agent actions, such as shield gates
rules.message: Hooks designed to block agent actions, such as shield gates

rules.lessonHeading: Constructing shell commands with string interpolation
rules.message: spawnSync called without an argument array — use spawnSync(cmd, [arg1, arg2], opts) instead of string interpolation to avoid shell injection and path-with-spaces bugs

rules.lessonHeading: Review metadata from third-party integrations
rules.message: Check that author field is not null/undefined before calling .includes() — third-party review metadata may have unexpected null author fields

rules.lessonHeading: Using child.kill() on Windows when shell: true is enabled
rules.message: Using child.kill() on Windows when shell: true is enabled

rules.lessonHeading: Technical documentation must avoid marketing-centric terms
rules.message: Technical documentation must avoid marketing-centric terms. Replace absolute claims with specific descriptions of tested environments and observed results.

rules.lessonHeading: Prefer Explicit Metadata Tokens from LLMs Over Heuristics
rules.message: Prefer Explicit Metadata Tokens from LLMs Over Heuristics

rules.lessonHeading: When timing out a child process, do not reject the promise
rules.message: When timing out a child process, do not reject the promise

rules.lessonHeading: Use the --recurse-submodules flag with git ls-files
rules.message: Use the --recurse-submodules flag with git ls-files

rules.lessonHeading: Resolve git root via rev-parse for monorepo compatibility
rules.message: Resolve git root via rev-parse for monorepo compatibility

rules.lessonHeading: When intercepting agent tool inputs via hooks, use specific
rules.message: When intercepting agent tool inputs via hooks, use specific

rules.lessonHeading: Use bracket notation for reserved keys
rules.message: Use bracket notation for reserved keys (e.g., payload['error'] = ...) to avoid lint rule violations instead of dot notation with inline suppressions.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid including empty strings in branch whitelists or using
rules.message: Empty string in array (possible branch whitelist) can silently bypass security gates if branch resolution fails. Remove the empty string entry.

rules.lessonHeading: Use maxRetries and retryDelay in fs.rmSync calls
rules.message: fs.rmSync calls should include maxRetries and retryDelay options to prevent ENOTEMPTY errors on Windows during test teardown

rules.lessonHeading: Eagerly importing heavy modules at the top level of CLI
rules.message: Avoid top-level static imports of heavy modules in CLI command files. Use dynamic import() inside handlers to reduce startup latency.

rules.lessonHeading: Removing stdio from execution options and hardcoding pipe
rules.message: Do not pass 'stdio' in execution options — remove it and rely on hardcoded pipe mode to ensure consistent string-based return values.

rules.lessonHeading: Escape Windows paths in generated configs
rules.message: When embedding paths in generated config files, escape backslashes (replace(/\\/g, '\\\\')) to prevent corruption on Windows

rules.lessonHeading: Using a unified 'Totem Error' tag across all CLI commands
rules.message: Use a unified 'Totem Error' tag prefix in CLI error output (e.g., console.error('[Totem Error]', ...)) for consistent log filtering

rules.lessonHeading: Pin Bun versions in CI
rules.message: Pin an explicit Bun version instead of 'latest' in CI to ensure reproducible builds and avoid unexpected breakages from runtime updates.

rules.lessonHeading: Orchestrators must dynamically adjust max_tokens based
rules.message: Hardcoded max_tokens value detected. Orchestrators must dynamically adjust max_tokens based on the specific model being used, as limits vary significantly (e.g., 4K for Haiku vs 16K for Opus). Use a model-aware lookup instead of a hardcoded literal.

rules.lessonHeading: Prefer exit codes over re-throwing CLI errors
rules.message: Prefer `log.error(...)` and `process.exitCode = 1` over re-throwing exceptions in CLI command handlers

rules.lessonHeading: Manual lessons must use the '## Lesson — <Heading>'
rules.message: Manual lessons must use the '## Lesson — <Heading>' structure (with an em dash '—') to ensure compatibility with automated parsing and discovery tools.

rules.lessonHeading: Verify CLI availability in shared git hooks before execution
rules.message: Verify CLI availability in shared git hooks before execution

rules.lessonHeading: CLI command modules should use dynamic imports for core
rules.message: CLI command modules should use dynamic imports (e.g., const { x } = await import('@core/...')) for core library values to avoid startup overhead.

rules.lessonHeading: Static analysis tools should read file content using git
rules.message: Static analysis tools should read file content using git

rules.lessonHeading: Always stage specific files instead of using 'git add -A'
rules.message: Avoid 'git add -A' or 'git add --all' in automated workflows — stage specific files explicitly to prevent accidental inclusion of unrelated changes

rules.lessonHeading: Global config paths must be absolute
rules.message: totemDir in global config must be an absolute path. Relative paths resolve relative to cwd and cause lookup failures.

rules.lessonHeading: Core libraries should avoid direct console.warn calls
rules.message: Core libraries should not use console.warn directly. Use injectable handlers or status objects instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Include a space or delimiter when concatenating disjoint
rules.message: Include a space or delimiter when concatenating disjoint

rules.lessonHeading: When injecting vectordb lessons into orchestrator prompts
rules.message: When injecting vectordb lessons into orchestrator prompts

rules.lessonHeading: When wrapping external errors or providing fallbacks,
rules.message: When wrapping external errors or providing fallbacks,

rules.lessonHeading: Swallowing errors in core modules hides silent failures,
rules.message: Avoid direct console.log in core modules. Use an optional onWarn callback to report diagnostics, keeping the library decoupled from the presentation layer.

rules.lessonHeading: System calls in automation scripts often fail silently
rules.message: Always check the result of spawnSync (e.g., .status, .error) to detect silent failures in system calls

rules.lessonHeading: Placing imports inside command function bodies prevents
rules.message: Do not use dynamic import() inside command function bodies — move imports to the top of the file to avoid hiding startup cost analysis, or ensure lazy loading is intentional and documented

rules.lessonHeading: The shell option in spawn() calls should be omitted
rules.message: The shell option in spawn() calls should be omitted

rules.lessonHeading: Using exact length assertions like toHaveLength() instead
rules.message: Use toHaveLength() instead of toBeGreaterThanOrEqual() for length assertions in tests — exact length checks catch over-splitting and empty segment regressions

rules.lessonHeading: Surface specific git fallback errors
rules.message: Avoid empty catch blocks in git fallback logic — surface specific errors (e.g., 'missing upstream') so users can diagnose configuration issues instead of seeing a generic 'no changes found' message.

rules.lessonHeading: Trimming output from commands like 'git ls-files -z'
rules.message: Do not pass trim: true when executing commands like 'git ls-files -z' or 'git show' — it can corrupt NUL-delimited paths or strip intentional whitespace. Use trim: false to preserve raw output.

rules.lessonHeading: Static top-level imports from heavy internal packages delay
rules.message: Static top-level imports from heavy internal packages delay

rules.lessonHeading: Using a boolean flag to track lazy initialization causes
rules.message: Avoid using a boolean flag for lazy initialization — store and return the initialization promise instead to prevent race conditions with concurrent callers (e.g., Promise.all).

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid forcing case normalization on file path filters
rules.message: Avoid forcing case normalization (e.g., toLowerCase/toUpperCase) on file path filters — this breaks case-sensitive filesystems (Linux/macOS) and may be unsupported by SQL dialects like LanceDB/DataFusion.

rules.lessonHeading: When mapping violations to rules in SARIF generation,
rules.message: When mapping violations to rules in SARIF generation,

rules.lessonHeading: Use bash [[ =~ ]] not echo | grep
rules.message: Use native bash [[ $var =~ pattern ]] instead of echo | grep for variable matching — avoids unnecessary subshells

rules.lessonHeading: When detecting markers in user-authored files, use
rules.message: When detecting markers in user-authored files, use

rules.lessonHeading: Using a boolean flag to track lazy initialization causes
rules.message: Using a boolean flag to track lazy initialization causes

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using dynamic imports in shared utility modules
rules.message: Avoid dynamic imports in shared utility modules. Place dynamic imports inside specific command handlers to defer heavy dependencies, not in shared utilities already in the main import tree.

rules.lessonHeading: Centralizing temporary directory cleanup into a helper
rules.message: Use the centralized temp directory cleanup helper (with retry logic) instead of calling fs.rmSync directly — prevents Windows file system flakes from race conditions or locked files.

rules.lessonHeading: When wrapping external errors or providing fallbacks,
rules.message: Ensure '[Totem Error]' prefix appears at the start of the error message string, not embedded mid-string

rules.lessonHeading: PID-based process liveness checks (process.kill(pid, 0))
rules.message: PID-based process liveness checks (process.kill(pid, 0))

rules.lessonHeading: Check for the presence of the 'g' flag before appending it
rules.message: Check for the presence of the 'g' flag before appending it

rules.lessonHeading: Dynamic imports should be limited to CLI command entry
rules.message: Dynamic import() detected outside CLI command entry points. Use static top-level imports in core utility/library code to avoid security scanner flags and maintain clean dependency graphs.

rules.lessonHeading: Raw child process output can be a Buffer or null; adding
rules.message: Child process stdout can be null; add a null/Buffer type guard before calling .toString().trim() to prevent runtime crashes.

rules.lessonHeading: When using stdio: 'pipe' with the GitHub CLI, set
rules.message: When using stdio: 'pipe' with the GitHub CLI (gh), set GH_PROMPT_DISABLED=1 in the env to prevent indefinite hangs from terminal input prompts.

rules.lessonHeading: Core library code should avoid direct calls to console
rules.message: Core library code should avoid direct calls to console

rules.lessonHeading: Custom .env parsers must strip CRLF and quotes
rules.message: Custom .env parsers must strip CRLF and quotes

rules.lessonHeading: Increase timeouts for Windows CI I/O
rules.message: Consider increasing test timeout to 15s for file-heavy tests on Windows CI (slow temp dir I/O can cause flaky failures with default 5s limits)

rules.lessonHeading: Dynamic imports should be limited to CLI command entry
rules.message: Dynamic imports should be limited to CLI command entry

rules.lessonHeading: GCA may suggest reverting dynamic imports back to static
rules.message: GCA may suggest reverting dynamic imports back to static

rules.lessonHeading: Use path.relative(process.cwd(),
rules.message: Use path.relative(process.cwd(),

rules.lessonHeading: Implementing a trap for EXIT, INT, and TERM signals ensures
rules.message: When creating temporary files with mktemp, ensure a trap is set for EXIT, INT, and TERM signals to guarantee cleanup on interruption.

rules.lessonHeading: Converting top-level static imports of heavy internal
rules.message: Avoid top-level static imports of heavy internal packages in CLI commands — use dynamic imports (e.g., const { x } = await import('...')) to reduce startup latency

rules.lessonHeading: Use strict boundaries (like (\/|$)) or the URL class
rules.message: Avoid prefix matching for loopback/local addresses — use strict boundaries like /(\/|$)/ or the URL class to prevent bypasses like 'localhost.evil.com'

rules.lessonHeading: Auto-formatters like Prettier can mangle technical metadata
rules.message: Escaped underscore or asterisk detected (e.g., `\_` or `\*`). Auto-formatters like Prettier may have mangled technical metadata — check that glob patterns and scope rules have not been corrupted.

rules.lessonHeading: Specific LLM models, such as Gemini's text-embedding-004,
rules.message: Specific LLM models, such as Gemini's text-embedding-004,

rules.lessonHeading: Restrict dynamic imports to CLI command entry points
rules.message: Dynamic imports are restricted to CLI command entry points. Use standard top-level imports in utility and adapter layers.

rules.lessonHeading: Explicitly verify that glob entries are strings
rules.message: Verify that the value is a string before calling .startsWith — arrays may contain non-string types, causing runtime crashes.

rules.lessonHeading: Loading large Tree-sitter WASM binaries at the module level
rules.message: Avoid importing Tree-sitter WASM binaries at the module level — use lazy/dynamic import() to prevent cold-start penalties in CLI tools

rules.lessonHeading: Project conventions require log.error to use a mandatory
rules.message: log.error must use the mandatory 'Totem Error' tag for system-wide consistency. Ensure the first argument or tag argument is 'Totem Error'.

rules.lessonHeading: Treat vector indexes as local-only caches that are excluded
rules.message: Vector index files should not be committed — treat them as local-only caches excluded from version control (add to .gitignore and rebuild via sync commands)

rules.lessonHeading: Git commands used for programmatic parsing should be
rules.message: Git commands used for programmatic parsing should be prefixed with LC_ALL=C to ensure locale-independent output (e.g., LC_ALL=C git log ...)

rules.lessonHeading: Metacharacters must be escaped when interpolating dynamic
rules.message: Escape metacharacters when interpolating dynamic strings into RegExp to prevent regex injection. Use a sanitization function (e.g., escapeRegExp) before passing dynamic input to new RegExp().

rules.lessonHeading: When identifying directory-based glob patterns (like
rules.message: When identifying directory-based glob patterns (like

rules.lessonHeading: Perform explicit null and type checks on the results
rules.message: Perform explicit null and type checks on the results

rules.lessonHeading: Brace expansion (e.g., \*.{ts,js}) is not universally
rules.message: Brace expansion (e.g., *.{ts,js}) is not universally supported and may silently match zero files. Expand into separate glob strings instead (e.g., ["**/*.ts", "**/*.js"]).

rules.lessonHeading: Using require() inside vi.mock callbacks can lead
rules.message: Do not use require() inside vi.mock callbacks — use vi.importActual or async imports for ESM-safe dependency resolution

rules.lessonHeading: git ls-files with relative paths fails from subdirs
rules.message: Use 'git -C "$REPO_ROOT"' before ls-files (or use absolute paths) to avoid failures when called from subdirectories

rules.lessonHeading: Using 'pnpm run version' instead of the bare 'pnpm version'
rules.message: Use 'pnpm run version' (not bare 'pnpm version') to correctly trigger the Changesets workflow and update changelogs.

rules.lessonHeading: Ensure model name validation regexes explicitly allow
rules.message: Ensure model name validation regexes explicitly allow

rules.lessonHeading: NEVER inline secrets, tokens, or API keys into agent config
rules.message: Never inline secrets/tokens/API keys in agent config files. Use gitignored .env files instead — MCP servers inherit env vars from the shell automatically.

rules.lessonHeading: LLMs are notoriously poor at character counting; use
rules.message: LLMs are notoriously poor at character counting; use

rules.lessonHeading: The Git -- separator treats all subsequent arguments
rules.message: The Git -- separator treats all subsequent arguments

rules.lessonHeading: Check for the presence of the global ('g') flag
rules.message: Check for the presence of the global ('g') flag

rules.lessonHeading: Use dynamic await import() inside CLI command handlers
rules.message: Use dynamic `await import()` inside CLI command handlers instead of top-level static imports for large internal packages. This keeps fast operations like --version and --help responsive.

rules.lessonHeading: Relying on error name strings for matching is fragile
rules.message: Avoid matching errors by name string — use instanceof with specific error codes for robust, type-safe error branching

rules.lessonHeading: Sanitize user-provided text before persisting to files
rules.message: Sanitize user-provided text before persisting to files

rules.lessonHeading: Static top-level imports from heavy internal packages delay
rules.message: Avoid static top-level imports from heavy internal packages in CLI commands. Use dynamic `await import(...)` inside the command function to avoid delaying startup for every invocation (including --help).

rules.lessonHeading: Use 'set -f' (noglob) when passing cached glob patterns
rules.message: Use 'set -f' (noglob) before passing cached glob patterns to git commands to prevent shell expansion of pathspecs into local filenames

rules.lessonHeading: Frontmatter delimiters must be anchored to the start
rules.message: Frontmatter delimiter '---' must be anchored to the start of the line (no leading whitespace)

rules.lessonHeading: Manually suppress "unused export" errors in styleguide
rules.message: Manually suppress "unused export" errors in styleguide

rules.lessonHeading: Omit shell:true when spawn args are arrays
rules.message: Omit shell:true when spawn args are already a parameterized array — it introduces shell injection risk and overhead

rules.lessonHeading: Reporting malformed records via an onWarn callback instead
rules.message: Do not silently swallow JSON parse errors in catch blocks — report malformed records via an onWarn callback instead of empty catch blocks

rules.lessonHeading: Diagnostic hints must precisely identify the underlying
rules.message: Use execFileSync instead of execSync to avoid shell-parsing overhead and security risks

rules.lessonHeading: Never use git add -A or git add .
rules.message: Never use 'git add -A' or 'git add .' — always stage specific files by name to avoid committing artifacts, lock files, or agent worktree copies

rules.lessonHeading: The LLM documentation generator consistently hallucinates
rules.message: Possible hallucination: Issue #515 (Claude Code hooks for spec preflight/shield pre-push) was closed as NOT implemented. Remove this reference. The hooks feature is tracked under #520 and is also not yet implemented.

rules.lessonHeading: Standard string length checks like .min(1) fail to catch
rules.message: Use .trim().min(N) instead of .min(N) alone in Zod schemas — .min() alone allows whitespace-only strings

rules.lessonHeading: Shell execution wrappers that automatically trim output
rules.message: Shell execution result may be undefined or a Buffer when stdio is 'inherit'/'ignore'. Check result type before calling string methods like .toString().trim().

rules.lessonHeading: Do not replace Zod validation with static type assertions
rules.message: Do not replace Zod validation with static type assertions for untrusted inputs — use Zod .parse() or .safeParse() instead. Type assertions provide zero runtime safety.

rules.lessonHeading: When re-throwing errors in a CLI orchestrator, always
rules.message: When re-throwing errors in a CLI orchestrator, always

rules.lessonHeading: Windows requires shell:true for git binary resolution
rules.message: Windows requires shell:true for git binary resolution

rules.lessonHeading: Use a '## Lesson — <Heading>' format and 'Tags' field
rules.message: Lesson headings must use the format '## Lesson — <Heading>' (with em dash and space)

rules.lessonHeading: Initialize WASM engines lazily
rules.message: Do not initialize WASM engines at the top level — move initialization into specific command handlers to avoid overhead on every CLI command (including 'help').

rules.lessonHeading: Use dynamic import() within CLI command handlers instead
rules.message: Use dynamic import() within CLI command handlers instead of top-level static imports to minimize startup latency.

rules.lessonHeading: Lesson headings must not exceed 60 characters
rules.message: Lesson heading exceeds 60 characters — headings must be ≤60 chars to serve as unique SARIF identifiers

rules.lessonHeading: Restricting dynamic imports to CLI command entry points
rules.message: Dynamic imports should only be used in CLI command entry points, not in utility or adapter layers.

rules.lessonHeading: GitHub Actions shell injection via template expressions
rules.message: GitHub Actions shell injection via template expressions

rules.lessonHeading: Setting open-pull-requests-limit to 0 in dependabot.yml
rules.message: Setting open-pull-requests-limit to 0 in dependabot.yml

rules.lessonHeading: Static top-level imports from heavy core packages
rules.message: Avoid static top-level imports from heavy core packages in CLI commands. Use dynamic `await import()` inside the command's execution function to avoid slowing down CLI startup.

rules.lessonHeading: Static analysis tools should read file content using git
rules.message: Static analysis tools should read file content using 'git show :path' to access the staged index version, not fs.readFile/readFileSync which reads unstaged disk content

rules.lessonHeading: Instead of hardcoding .git/hooks, use 'git rev-parse
rules.message: Do not hardcode '.git/hooks' — use 'git rev-parse --git-path hooks' to support custom hook paths and worktrees correctly.

rules.lessonHeading: When detecting markers in user-authored files, use
rules.message: Use line-start regex (/^marker/m) instead of String.includes() when detecting markers in user-authored files to prevent false matches within content body

rules.lessonHeading: Core library modules should use optional callbacks
rules.message: Core library modules should not use hardcoded console.warn — use an optional onWarn callback instead to remain environment-agnostic

rules.lessonHeading: README and curated wiki pages must never be LLM-generated
rules.message: Protected documents (README.md, COVENANT.md, CHANGELOG.md, wiki pages) must not appear in the totem docs array — they must be handcrafted, not LLM-generated

rules.lessonHeading: Silently ignoring Git checkout errors in cleanup blocks
rules.message: Do not silently ignore errors in catch blocks — especially git checkout failures in cleanup/finally blocks. Always log a warning with recovery instructions.

rules.lessonHeading: The use of dynamic imports inside CLI command handlers
rules.message: Dynamic imports inside CLI command handlers are required to ensure heavy dependencies load only when the command is executed, keeping initial boot time fast.

rules.lessonHeading: Ensure background hooks use piped stdio and disabled
rules.message: Background hooks spawned in git lifecycle events must use stdio: 'pipe' and disable interactive prompts to prevent terminal pollution or hung processes.

rules.lessonHeading: Performing content.split('\n') inside a loop over line
rules.message: Performing content.split('\n') inside a loop over line

rules.lessonHeading: Reject non-positive PIDs and infinite timestamps
rules.message: Do not call process.kill(0, ...) — validate that PID is positive before signaling to avoid accidental process group signals

rules.lessonHeading: Use centralized utilities for regex escaping
rules.message: Use the centralized escapeRegex utility when building RegExp from dynamic config to prevent regex injection vulnerabilities.

rules.lessonHeading: File system watchers should monitor for "rename" events
rules.message: File system watchers should handle 'rename' events to support atomic saves by editors. Check that the event handler processes 'rename' in addition to 'change'.

rules.lessonHeading: For data structures with few fields (e.g., < 10), use type
rules.message: For small data structures (< 10 fields), prefer TypeScript 'type' aliases over Zod schemas to avoid unnecessary dependency overhead in the CLI.

rules.lessonHeading: Binaries like git may fail to resolve correctly when using
rules.message: execFileSync may fail to resolve binaries like 'git' on Windows without shell: true. Add { shell: true } to the options.

rules.lessonHeading: Exclude test files from XSS rules
rules.message: Avoid assigning to innerHTML directly — this is a dangerous XSS sink. If this is test code, move it to a test file (*.test.ts / *.spec.ts) which is excluded from this rule.

rules.lessonHeading: Simple string lookups like indexOf('[{') fail to detect
rules.message: indexOf('[{') fails to detect pretty-printed JSON arrays. Use a regex search for /\[\s*{/ to correctly identify array starts regardless of whitespace.

rules.lessonHeading: Replacing direct process.exit(1) calls with thrown custom
rules.message: Avoid direct process.exit(1) calls — throw a custom error instead and handle exit codes at the top-level application boundary to keep command logic composable and testable.

rules.lessonHeading: Use fixed literal tags for log.error calls
rules.message: Use a fixed literal string tag (e.g., 'Totem Error') in log.error calls instead of a variable constant, to ensure consistent and filterable error reporting.

rules.lessonHeading: Use explicit comments or trace logs when catch blocks
rules.message: Catch block used as a logic fallback should contain an explicit comment or trace/log call to distinguish intentional fallback from silent error swallowing.

rules.lessonHeading: Explicit type assertions are often unnecessary when using
rules.message: Explicit type assertion after nullish coalescing with a literal fallback is unnecessary — TypeScript infers the narrowest type automatically.

rules.lessonHeading: When identifying files by naming conventions (e.g.,
rules.message: Avoid using path.includes() to match file naming conventions (e.g., '.test.', '.spec.'). Use path.basename() first to avoid false positives from directory segments matching the pattern.

rules.lessonHeading: Top-level imports of heavy classes or modules
rules.message: Avoid top-level imports of heavy modules in CLI commands. Use dynamic imports (e.g., `const { X } = await import('...')`) inside the command function body to preserve fast startup performance.

rules.lessonHeading: Silent failures in 'best-effort' operations like staleness
rules.message: Empty catch block silently swallows errors. Use non-blocking logging (e.g., console.warn or logger) to keep failures traceable without crashing execution.

rules.lessonHeading: Empty catch blocks during file discovery can lead
rules.message: Empty catch block detected: swallowed errors during file discovery can cause incomplete data sets and incorrect downstream logic (e.g., false 'missing' signals). Add explicit error handling to distinguish missing directories from read failures.

rules.lessonHeading: Escape backticks in SQL predicates
rules.message: Ensure backticks are escaped alongside single quotes in SQL predicate/LIKE clause sanitization to prevent identifier-based injection attacks

rules.lessonHeading: Technical documentation must replace absolute claims
rules.message: Avoid absolute claims like 'guarantee', 'comprehensive', 'always', 'never', 'perfect', 'complete', 'foolproof', or '100% reliable' in technical documentation. Replace with specific, evidence-backed descriptions of tested behavior.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using partial matchers like toContain when testing
rules.message: Avoid toContain for testing secret masking or data sanitization — use exact string assertions (e.g., toBe or toEqual) to ensure surrounding context is preserved and nothing is over-redacted.

rules.lessonHeading: Explicit type assertions are often unnecessary when using
rules.message: Explicit type assertions are often unnecessary when using

rules.lessonHeading: Standardize exception messages with a consistent prefix
rules.message: Standardize exception messages with a consistent prefix

rules.lessonHeading: Always iterate through all regex matches (e.g.,
rules.message: Always iterate through all regex matches (e.g.,

rules.lessonHeading: Use --body-file for LLM text in CLI
rules.message: Use --body-file for LLM text in CLI

rules.lessonHeading: While using shell: true fixes Windows ENOENT errors
rules.message: Avoid shell: true in spawn — manually append .cmd to the executable instead to fix Windows ENOENT errors without shell injection risks

rules.lessonHeading: AI tool metadata must treat MCP-related fields as optional
rules.message: AI tool metadata must treat MCP-related fields as optional

rules.lessonHeading: Confine dynamic imports to CLI command handlers to maintain
rules.message: Dynamic imports must be confined to CLI command handlers (packages/cli/src/commands/). Move this dynamic import into a command handler file.

rules.lessonHeading: Prefer totem lint over the deprecated totem shield
rules.message: Use 'totem lint' instead of the deprecated 'totem shield --deterministic' for zero-LLM structural validation

rules.lessonHeading: Sanitize git-sourced metadata like branch names, status,
rules.message: Sanitize git-sourced metadata like branch names, status,

rules.lessonHeading: When a hook is intended to run unconditionally, use
rules.message: Use '**/*' instead of an empty string for unconditional hook glob patterns to clarify intent

rules.lessonHeading: Using .includes() to identify target files like READMEs
rules.message: Avoid using .includes() to identify target files (e.g., READMEs) — use path.basename() with case-insensitive comparison to avoid false positives from directory paths or similar filenames.

rules.lessonHeading: CLI entrypoints print clean errors, libraries throw
rules.message: CLI entrypoints print clean errors, libraries throw

rules.lessonHeading: Use process.exitCode for CLI exits
rules.message: Prefer setting process.exitCode instead of calling process.exit() to allow graceful shutdown (cleanup, log flushing, child process termination).

rules.lessonHeading: CLI tools must check for an existing .git directory
rules.message: CLI tools must check for an existing .git directory before running git init. Blindly executing git init can corrupt existing repositories or submodules.

rules.lessonHeading: Git output can vary by system locale, causing parsing logic
rules.message: Git commands should be run with LC_ALL=C to ensure consistent output format across different system locales

rules.lessonHeading: Using Array.prototype.find inside a loop results in O(N\*M)
rules.message: Avoid Array.prototype.find inside a loop (O(N*M) complexity). Index data into a Map before the loop for O(1) lookups.

rules.lessonHeading: Functions with 'OrExit' suffixes must actually terminate
rules.message: Functions with 'OrExit' suffix must call process.exit() on failure, not return empty results

rules.lessonHeading: Orchestrators must dynamically adjust max_tokens based
rules.message: Orchestrators must dynamically adjust max_tokens based

rules.lessonHeading: Reusing regex objects with the global flag in loops leads
rules.message: Avoid reusing regex objects with the global flag across iterations. Instantiate a fresh RegExp inside the loop or reset lastIndex to 0 before each use.

rules.lessonHeading: The Gemini CLI and Gemini Code Assist (GCA) do not
rules.message: The Gemini CLI and Gemini Code Assist (GCA) do not

rules.lessonHeading: Exported configuration structures like command groups
rules.message: Exported configuration structures (like command groups) should use ReadonlySet<T> instead of Set<T> to prevent accidental runtime mutations.

rules.lessonHeading: CLI command entrypoints should catch validation errors
rules.message: CLI command entrypoints should catch validation errors

rules.lessonHeading: Resolve persistent directories like caches relative
rules.message: Resolve persistent directories (caches, etc.) relative to the config file's location, not process.cwd(). Using process.cwd() creates orphaned directories when the tool runs from different subdirectories.

rules.lessonHeading: Dynamic imports used for CLI startup performance should be
rules.message: Dynamic import() found outside a command entry point. Dynamic imports for CLI startup performance should be restricted to command entry points only, to maintain a clean dependency graph and pass security scans in utility layers.

rules.lessonHeading: Using simple substring matching like "git" && "commit"
rules.message: Avoid simple substring matching with "git" && "commit" in shell hooks — use order-sensitive regex (e.g., /^git commit/) to prevent false positives on unrelated commands

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using Zod's .url() validator for configurations where
rules.message: Avoid using Zod's .url() validator for configurations where

rules.lessonHeading: Utilize jq instead of standard string manipulation tools
rules.message: Use jq instead of grep/sed for JSON data extraction — it is more reliable for structured data

rules.lessonHeading: Use byte-level binary size checks
rules.message: Use byte-level size checks (du -b or wc -c) instead of megabyte-level (du -m) to prevent binaries near the threshold from bypassing size limits.

rules.lessonHeading: Ensure all thrown errors, including those for missing
rules.message: Ensure all thrown errors, including those for missing

rules.lessonHeading: Issue numbers are only unique within a single repository;
rules.message: Issue numbers are only unique within a single repository;

rules.lessonHeading: When redirecting stdio to a file for a detached process
rules.message: When using fs.openSync for detached process stdio redirection, ensure the file descriptor is explicitly closed in the parent process (e.g., fs.closeSync(fd)) to prevent resource leaks.

rules.lessonHeading: The text-embedding-004 identifier is frequently unavailable
rules.message: The text-embedding-004 identifier is frequently unavailable

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid inlining tokens or API keys in configuration files
rules.message: Avoid inlining tokens or API keys in configuration files

rules.lessonHeading: Standardized logging utilities should always be used over
rules.message: Use the internal 'log' utility instead of console.error to ensure consistent output formatting and integration with the project's tagging system.

rules.lessonHeading: The LLM documentation generator consistently hallucinates
rules.message: The LLM documentation generator consistently hallucinates

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using console methods directly in core library
rules.message: Avoid using console methods directly in core library

rules.lessonHeading: When manually parsing CLI arguments, verify that a flag's
rules.message: When manually parsing CLI arguments, verify that a flag's

rules.lessonHeading: The @ast-grep/napi findAll method accepts both string
rules.message: The @ast-grep/napi findAll method accepts both string

rules.lessonHeading: Operations like store.count() can throw after heavy inserts
rules.message: store.count() can throw after heavy inserts or FTS rebuilding — wrap in try/catch to avoid reporting a successful sync as a failure

rules.lessonHeading: Using split() on markdown headings often misclassifies
rules.message: Using split() on markdown headings often misclassifies

rules.lessonHeading: Set LLM temperature to zero during build
rules.message: LLM calls during build/rule-compilation must use temperature: 0 to ensure deterministic output and valid manifest hashes.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid bypassing visibility checks with bracket notation
rules.message: Avoid bypassing visibility checks with bracket notation

rules.lessonHeading: The fileGlobs array can contain objects for ast-grep rules,
rules.message: fileGlobs entries may be objects, not just strings — verify the entry is a string before calling startsWith

rules.lessonHeading: Spying on underlying implementation details
rules.message: Do not spy on console methods directly — spy on the project's public logging utility instead to avoid fragile tests tied to internal implementation details.

rules.lessonHeading: Use byte-level binary size checks
rules.message: Use byte-level binary size checks (e.g., `du -b` or `wc -c`) rather than megabyte integers to avoid truncation errors near the size limit

rules.lessonHeading: Node's spawn() emits resolution errors via the 'error'
rules.message: spawn() errors (e.g. command-not-found) are emitted via the 'error' event on the child process, not catchable by try/catch. Listen to childProcess.on('error', ...) instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Using synchronous file operations like fs.readFileSync
rules.message: Avoid fs.readFileSync — use fs.promises.readFile instead to prevent blocking the Node.js event loop

rules.lessonHeading: Dynamic imports prevent heavy dependencies from degrading
rules.message: Dynamic imports should only be used in command entry points, not in utility or adapter layers. Move this dynamic import to the command entry point to prevent startup latency issues.

rules.lessonHeading: Do not manually edit machine-generated artifacts
rules.message: Do not manually edit machine-generated artifacts

rules.lessonHeading: Convert top-level static imports to dynamic import() calls
rules.message: Convert top-level static imports to dynamic import() calls inside command handler functions to reduce CLI startup latency.

rules.lessonHeading: Automated gates like pre-commit or pre-push hooks
rules.message: Automated gates like pre-commit or pre-push hooks

rules.lessonHeading: Using child.kill() on Windows when shell: true is enabled
rules.message: child.kill() on Windows with shell:true only kills the cmd.exe wrapper, leaving zombie processes. Use taskkill /T /F to kill the entire process tree.

rules.lessonHeading: AI agent memory config architectures differ significantly
rules.message: AI agent memory config architectures differ significantly

rules.lessonHeading: Moving static imports of heavy internal packages
rules.message: Avoid static imports of heavy internal packages in command handlers. Use dynamic `await import()` inside the command handler to reduce CLI startup latency.

rules.lessonHeading: Hardcoding specific filenames like active_work.md
rules.message: Do not hardcode 'active_work.md' in document-processing logic; use a configuration flag or variable to define the document target instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Generating IDs from violation properties like file, line,
rules.message: Generating IDs from violation properties like file, line,

rules.lessonHeading: Named constants for file permission literals
rules.message: Use a named constant instead of an inline octal literal for file permissions (e.g., const FILE_PERMISSIONS = 0o644). Inline magic numbers make intent unclear and harder to audit.

rules.lessonHeading: Detect existing hook managers and provide manual guidance
rules.message: Detect existing hook managers and provide manual guidance

rules.lessonHeading: When catching and re-logging errors that originate
rules.message: When catching and re-logging errors that originate

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid bypassing visibility checks with bracket notation
rules.message: Avoid using bracket notation to call methods — this bypasses TypeScript visibility checks. Use a public static factory method instead.

rules.lessonHeading: Escape metacharacters in RegExp interpolation
rules.message: Escape metacharacters when interpolating dynamic strings into RegExp — use a helper like escapeRegExp(str) to prevent regex injection or catastrophic backtracking.

rules.lessonHeading: Always verify that both stdin and stdout are TTY-enabled
rules.message: Always check both process.stdin.isTTY AND process.stdout.isTTY before launching interactive prompts — checking only one is insufficient

rules.lessonHeading: Wrap user-controlled fields like PR descriptions
rules.message: Wrap user-controlled fields like PR descriptions

rules.lessonHeading: GitHub API responses often contain \r\n line endings, so
rules.message: Regex patterns matching newlines should use \r?\n instead of \n to handle Windows-style (CRLF) line endings from GitHub API responses

rules.lessonHeading: When scanning directories for feature detection
rules.message: Empty catch block swallows all errors — explicitly handle ENOENT and rethrow or log other system errors to avoid masking permission issues or structural problems.

rules.lessonHeading: Git diff headers wrap file paths containing spaces
rules.message: Git diff headers wrap file paths containing spaces

rules.lessonHeading: Synchronous file operations are often preferable in CLI
rules.message: Synchronous file operations are often preferable in CLI

rules.lessonHeading: Environment variables from the host machine can leak
rules.message: Accessing process.env in tests may leak host environment variables. Explicitly mock or clear sensitive env vars (e.g., API keys) in test setup to avoid non-deterministic behavior.

rules.lessonHeading: NEVER inline secrets, tokens, or API keys into agent config
rules.message: NEVER inline secrets, tokens, or API keys into agent config

rules.lessonHeading: Omit defensive XML escaping for prompt injection
rules.message: Omit defensive XML escaping for prompt injection

rules.lessonHeading: Use exit codes for CLI errors
rules.message: Prefer setting process.exitCode and logging via log.error over re-throwing exceptions in CLI command handlers

rules.lessonHeading: Treat usage and token statistics as optional fields
rules.message: Treat usage and token statistics as optional fields

rules.lessonHeading: When normalizing diverse SDK errors for internal retry
rules.message: When normalizing diverse SDK errors for internal retry

rules.lessonHeading: Handle dollar signs in identifier boundaries
rules.message: Standard \b word boundaries fail for JS identifiers containing '$'. Use (?:^|[^\w$]) as a prefix/suffix to correctly isolate identifiers with dollar signs.

rules.lessonHeading: Core packages should provide a logging interface and DI
rules.message: Core packages must not call console methods directly. Provide a logging interface/DI contract instead to prevent stdout pollution in non-CLI environments.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid hardcoding .git/hooks paths as repositories may use
rules.message: Avoid hardcoding .git/hooks paths — repositories may use custom core.hooksPath. Use `git rev-parse --git-path hooks` for reliable resolution.

rules.lessonHeading: Use maxRetries and retryDelay in fs.rmSync to prevent
rules.message: fs.rmSync should include maxRetries and retryDelay options to prevent ENOTEMPTY flakes on Windows during rapid test teardowns.

rules.lessonHeading: Preserve error context when re-throwing
rules.message: Preserve original error context when re-throwing after JSON.parse — include the caught error (e.g., { cause: err } or err.message) so the root cause is not swallowed.
rules.astGrepPattern: try { $$$PRE; JSON.parse($INPUT); $$$POST } catch ($ERR) { throw new Error($MSG) }

rules.lessonHeading: Individual CLI commands should throw standardized errors
rules.message: CLI commands should throw standardized errors instead of logging locally with console.error — let the central top-level handler manage error formatting and exit codes

rules.lessonHeading: Use anchored regex for shell command matching
rules.message: Use anchored regex (e.g., /^\s*git\s+/) for shell command matching to avoid false positives on paths or comments

rules.lessonHeading: Prefer shell redirection over cat pipe
rules.message: Prefer shell redirection (< file) over 'cat file |' to avoid spawning an unnecessary process (useless use of cat)

rules.lessonHeading: Stripping non-ASCII characters during keyword extraction
rules.message: Stripping non-ASCII characters during keyword extraction causes pattern ID collisions for non-English messages. Use Unicode property escapes instead to ensure unique identification across languages.

rules.lessonHeading: Set higher timeouts for orchestrator tests
rules.message: Orchestrator test suites should set an explicit 15s timeout (e.g., { timeout: 15000 }) to prevent flaky CI failures during heavy workloads

rules.lessonHeading: Always generate sentinel markers even when the internal
rules.message: Always generate sentinel markers even when the internal

rules.lessonHeading: Re-thrown errors must preserve the original cause
rules.message: Re-thrown errors must preserve the original cause

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using console methods directly in core library
rules.message: Avoid using console methods directly in core library modules. Provide an optional `onWarn` callback instead, so consuming applications can decide how to surface warnings.

rules.lessonHeading: Always use dynamic imports for heavy logic or detection
rules.message: Use dynamic imports (await import(...)) for heavy logic or detection modules in CLI command functions to ensure fast startup performance.

rules.lessonHeading: Shell hooks should use word-boundary regex instead
rules.message: Use word-boundary regex (e.g., [[ "$cmd" =~ \bcommit\b ]]) instead of simple string equality to match git subcommands — prevents false positives and correctly handles flags like 'git -c ... commit'

rules.lessonHeading: Core packages should use an injectable logger interface
rules.message: Core packages should use an injectable logger interface instead of direct console calls, to allow consumers to control output routing.

rules.lessonHeading: The text-embedding-004 identifier is frequently unavailable
rules.message: Use 'gemini-embedding-2-preview' instead of 'text-embedding-004' — the latter is frequently unavailable and causes API initialization failures

rules.lessonHeading: Junie requires specific file locations for auto-detection,
rules.message: Junie requires specific file locations for auto-detection,

rules.lessonHeading: Calling .test() on a global regex increments the lastIndex
rules.message: Calling .test() on a global regex increments lastIndex, which can cause subsequent replacements to skip characters and bypass sanitization. Use a non-global regex for .test(), or reset lastIndex before use.

rules.lessonHeading: Static top-level imports in CLI command files increase
rules.message: Avoid static top-level imports of heavy schemas or internal packages in CLI command files. Use dynamic imports inside command handlers to reduce startup latency.

rules.lessonHeading: When testing expected failures, assert the specific error
rules.message: Assert the specific error message or type in rejects.toThrow() — bare toThrow() allows unrelated errors to pass the test

rules.lessonHeading: Casting an object literal to an Error type does not satisfy
rules.message: Casting an object literal to an Error type does not satisfy

rules.lessonHeading: Background processes spawned by Git hooks should redirect
rules.message: Background processes in Git hooks must redirect stdout and stderr to a log file (e.g., >> logfile 2>&1 &) to prevent output from polluting the terminal mid-git-operation.

rules.lessonHeading: Marking test callbacks as async and awaiting synchronous
rules.message: Do not mark test callbacks as async unless the code under test is genuinely async — async wrappers mask synchronous API signatures and hide accidental Promise conversions

rules.lessonHeading: Do not include empty strings in branch whitelists,
rules.message: Do not include empty strings in branch whitelists — empty strings can accidentally match on environment detection failures, causing a hard block to be bypassed.

rules.lessonHeading: Throwing specific error classes like TotemParseError
rules.message: Avoid calling process.exit() directly. Throw a specific error class (e.g., TotemParseError) instead to ensure consistent error handling and exit behavior across the CLI.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid using dynamic imports in shared utility modules
rules.message: Avoid using dynamic imports in shared utility modules

rules.lessonHeading: Ensure issue numbers are non-empty digit strings
rules.message: Issue number after 'Deferred to #' must be a non-empty digit string — malformed markers break idempotency checks

rules.lessonHeading: Core library code should avoid direct calls to console
rules.message: Core library code should not call console directly — use a configurable callback (defaulting to no-op) so consumers control log routing

rules.lessonHeading: Perform shell-level existence checks before invoking CLI
rules.message: Perform shell-level existence checks before invoking CLI

rules.lessonHeading: Using empty catch blocks during regex compilation in audit
rules.message: Empty catch block silently swallows RegExp compilation errors in audit scripts — log or rethrow the error to surface malformed rules.

rules.lessonHeading: MCP session lifecycle limitation
rules.message: MCP session lifecycle limitation

rules.lessonHeading: Eagerly importing heavy modules at the top level of CLI
rules.message: Avoid eagerly importing heavy modules at the top level of CLI command files. Use dynamic import() inside handlers to keep startup fast.

rules.lessonHeading: Changesets interactive CLI (pnpm changeset) crashes when
rules.message: Changesets interactive CLI (pnpm changeset) crashes when

rules.lessonHeading: Forbid direct child_process — use safeExec
rules.message: Forbid direct child_process — use safeExec

rules.lessonHeading: Forbid inline JSON.parse(readFileSync) — use readJsonSafe
rules.message: Forbid inline JSON.parse(readFileSync) — use readJsonSafe

rules.lessonHeading: Use git adapter instead of raw git execution
rules.message: Use git adapter instead of raw git execution

rules.lessonHeading: Key security audit findings and acceptable risks
rules.message: Using shell: true with spawn can prevent proper process tree termination on Unix. Use detached: true + process.kill(-pid) for process group termination, and avoid shell: true with array args.

rules.lessonHeading: Emojis are excluded from all documentation files to adhere
rules.message: Emojis are not allowed in documentation files — project standards require professional formatting without emoji characters

rules.lessonHeading: Implementing bypass logic via GitHub Actions contains()
rules.message: Use GitHub Actions contains() expressions (e.g., contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'label')) instead of shell-based parsing of PR metadata to avoid shell injection surfaces.

rules.lessonHeading: Redirecting hook command output to /dev/null prevents users
rules.message: Redirecting hook command output to /dev/null hides diagnostic information from users. Provide specific error messages or commands instead.
Always use the '--' separator before positional arguments in git commands (e.g., 'git log -- <ref>') to prevent argument injection of untrusted strings like branch or tag names.

rules.lessonHeading: Avoid inlining tokens or API keys in configuration files
rules.message: Do not inline tokens or API keys in configuration files. Use a gitignored .env file instead — MCP servers inherit environment variables from the parent shell.

rules.lessonHeading: Standardize catch block error naming
rules.message: Use 'err' as the catch block variable name (project convention), not '$ERROR'

rules.lessonHeading: Strip LLM-generated markdown wrappers
rules.message: Strip LLM-generated markdown wrappers (backticks/code fences) before parsing LLM output — raw JSON.parse on LLM responses may silently fail if markdown is present

rules.lessonHeading: Validate single-backtick wrappers before stripping
rules.message: Unconditionally stripping single backticks can corrupt patterns with internal backticks. Use a strict regex (e.g., /^`([^`]*)`$/) to validate that the backticks are actually external wrappers before stripping.

rules.lessonHeading: Prefer semantic limits over line counts
rules.message: Prefer character or directive counts over line counts for LLM instruction limits — LLMs consume tokens and semantic density, not physical lines.

rules.lessonHeading: Guard against conflicting upgrade and force flags
rules.message: Guard against conflicting --upgrade and --force flags: explicitly reject their combination to prevent cache corruption

rules.lessonHeading: Prefer in-process CLI command execution
rules.message: Prefer in-process CLI command execution over spawning a shell process. Call the command function directly instead of using spawn().

rules.lessonHeading: GitHub Closes keyword only applies to the first issue
rules.message: GitHub Closes keyword only applies to the first issue

rules.lessonHeading: Do not delete .totem/lessons.md (load-bearing for 41+ rules)
rules.message: Do not delete .totem/lessons.md (load-bearing for 41+ rules)

rules.lessonHeading: Proactively remove references to planned features from user
rules.message: Proactively remove references to planned features from user
rules.pattern: \b(coming soon|planned (feature|for)|future release|roadmap|under development|slated for|later development phases)\b
